pages.raw_text:
From intimations let out by men
connected with the survey of the
new Joint railroad between SDo-
kane and Pasco to be used by both
the Great Northern and tne North
ern Pacific, there is light thrown
on the surveys west of Monroe
street and north of the river bank.
II Is stated by officials of the lat
ter road that the question Of
change of grade of the Northern
Pacific in this city will not be de-
termined until the route of en-
trance Of the joint road into Spo-
kane is settled.


1t is now considered that the
solution lies in utilizing the north
bank Of the river along the line Of
the Great Northern toward the
mouth of Hangman creek. This
would afford method of transfer
ring cars from the Great Northern
to the new joint road. If such a
route is deemed feasible, it is just
as possible that the Northern Pa
ciflc traffic might be diverted from
its present tracks on the south side
to this new route.


The new route would enter the
city on a grade the most easy to
be found, which would be much
